hook: Sam, your rent and your patients' coverage are on the same ballot—and fewer than half of registered voters are showing up to decide.

because.text: you should vote because the Lieutenant Governor race was decided by just 399,125 votes (5.3%) in 2022, and that office controls which housing and healthcare bills even reach the Senate floor
you should vote because only 45.7% of registered voters turned out in 2022, meaning renters and healthcare workers like you were underrepresented in the outcome

you should vote because the Governor's race candidates diverge by 70 points on housing—one prioritizes property tax relief for homeowners, the other tenant protections and subsidies that directly affect your situation as a renter

source.title: Lt. Gov. Dan Patrick says his plan can eliminate school property taxes for homesteads in several sessions

because.text: you should vote because the Governor's race candidates diverge by 85 points on healthcare, with options ranging from full government-guaranteed coverage to Medicaid expansion—critical for your patients and your profession

source.title: Healthcare - Clayton Tucker for TX Ag Commissioner

if_you_vote: Your vote joins a pool that decided the last Governor's race by 883,443 votes and the Senate race by 959,492 votes—margins that shrink when turnout stays below 46%. On your priorities: the Governor appoints the Secretary of State and controls the budget veto, directly shaping whether Medicaid expansion reaches your patients; the Lieutenant Governor decides if tenant protection bills ever get a vote.
long_term: The 2026 Governor result compounds into 2027-2028: continued non-expansion means more rural hospital closures and Texas keeping the nation's highest uninsured rate, or Medicaid expansion implementation fights in the legislature that set the floor for 2028 federal healthcare negotiations. In housing, the Lieutenant Governor elected now controls the 2027-2028 legislative calendar—determining whether renter protections advance before the 2030 redistricting cycle locks in representation.
cta: Make your plan now: check your registration at votetexas.gov, mark November 3, 2026, and decide when you'll vote.
